techcrunch.title: As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future
techcrunch.summary: Tech leaders debate whether the Anthropic episode is a wake-up call for India’s AI ambitions.

techcrunch.title: Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2B Manus deal after Beijing’s demand
techcrunch.summary: Meta starts dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition after Beijing ordered the deal reversed.

techcrunch.title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations
techcrunch.summary: Once again, AI proves to be an unreliable source of information about AI.

techcrunch.title: Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before government crackdown
techcrunch.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy may have been the source of security concerns that led Anthropic to cut off worldwide access to two models on Friday.

techcrunch.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
techcrunch.summary: It's not clear which states are involved, but they're asking about everything from OpenAI's ad policies to its handling of health data.

techcrunch.title: Andrew Yang thinks the next big startup opportunity is lowering the cost of living
techcrunch.summary: Andrew Yang made a list of everything Americans overpay for — housing, food, wireless — and thinks the next startup gold rush is giving that money back.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
techcrunch.summary: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.

techcrunch.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
techcrunch.summary: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

techcrunch.title: Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it
techcrunch.summary: A new report suggests the unit, which employs 6,500 people, is on the verge of revolt.

techcrunch.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
techcrunch.summary: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.
